_comment: Trace where the bogus SIGCHLD handler 0xa000411f1 comes from. Break at musl's sigaction wrapper right where it reads sa->sa_handler, before writing ksa. The user's sigaction call in openrc passes the handler in the struct sigaction pointed to by RSI. Break at musl's inner sigaction at offset 0x5de6a (mov (%rsi),%rdx — reads sa->sa_handler into RDX).

steps.name: sigaction_reads_handler
steps._comment: musl at 0x5de6a: mov (%rsi),%rdx — loads sa->sa_handler. R8=user sa ptr. RDI=signal. Break here and check if RDX gets the bogus value. Use ld-musl base 0xa0000b000 for openrc.
steps._comment2: Actually, we can't break in userspace. Break at handle_user_fault (the crash) and then examine the PtRegs to find the user call stack. Look at the stack contents at RSP=0x9ffffe960 to find the caller.
